languageModelTools.name: list_scratches
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Lists all scratch files in the user's scratch storage, optionally filtered.
Use when user asks to see available scratches or find files by pattern or directory.

Parameters:
- filter (optional): A glob pattern, a bare basename, a path prefix, or a scratch:/// URI.
  - Glob pattern: any string containing *, ?, {, or [ (e.g. "**/*.ts", "projects/foo/**", "*.md")
    Glob patterns that do not already start with "**/" are automatically prepended with "**/",
    so all globs match recursively across the full tree (e.g. "*.md" matches all .md files, not just root-level).
  - Bare basename: a single name with no "/" (e.g. "README.md", "testing") is expanded to "**/name"
    and matches files with that name at any depth.
  - Path prefix: a slash-separated relative path (e.g. "projects/foo/", "projects/foo") matches all
    scratch paths that start with it. Trailing slash is optional.
  - scratch:/// URI: scheme is stripped and the appropriate rule above is applied.
  All forms match against the root-relative path without a leading slash.

Examples:
- {} → lists all scratches
- { filter: "projects/my-app/" } → all scratches under projects/my-app/
- { filter: "projects/my-app" } → same as above, trailing slash is optional
- { filter: "scratch:///projects/my-app" } → same, using scratch:/// URI
- { filter: "**/*.ts" } → all TypeScript files recursively
- { filter: "*.ts" } → same as **/*.ts, auto-expanded to match recursively
- { filter: "README.md" } → all files named README.md anywhere in the tree
- { filter: "projects/foo/**" } → all files under projects/foo/ via glob

Returns: Newline-separated paths (without leading slash), e.g. 'projects/foo/notes.md'. To use a path as a URI in other tools (read_scratch, get_scratch_outline, etc.), prefix it with 'scratch:///', e.g. 'scratch:///projects/foo/notes.md'.

filter.description: Filter to use when listing scratches. Accepts a glob pattern (e.g. "*.md", "projects/**"), a bare basename with no "/" (e.g. "README.md" — matched recursively at any depth), a path prefix (e.g. "projects/foo"), or a scratch:/// URI. Glob patterns and bare basenames without a leading **/ are automatically matched recursively. If omitted, all scratches are listed. Only use if a specific directory or set of files is needed, otherwise prefer leaving it empty.

languageModelTools.name: read_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reads content from a scratch file
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Reads content from one or more scratch files, optionally with line ranges per file. Batch multiple reads to minimize round-trips.
Use when user asks to view or retrieve scratch content. Combine with get_scratch_outline to read targeted sections by line range.

Parameters:
- reads (required): Array of read requests, each with:
  - uri (required): Scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///notes.md") or URI string
  - lineFrom (optional): Starting line number (1-based, inclusive, counting all lines including blank ones). Matches physical line numbers reported by get_scratch_outline.
  - lineTo (optional): Ending line number (1-based, inclusive, counting all lines including blank ones). Matches physical line numbers reported by get_scratch_outline.

Examples:
- { reads: [{ uri: "scratch:///notes.md" }] } → full file content
- { reads: [{ uri: "scratch:///code.ts", lineFrom: 10, lineTo: 20 }] } → lines 10 through 20 inclusive (blank lines counted)
- { reads: [{ uri: "scratch:///a.md", lineFrom: 5 }, { uri: "scratch:///b.md", lineTo: 10 }] } → batch read from two files
- { reads: [{ uri: "scratch:///notes.md", lineFrom: 1, lineTo: 5 }, { uri: "scratch:///notes.md", lineFrom: 20, lineTo: 30 }] } → two ranges from same file

Returns: Each result labeled with its URI and range. Multiple results separated by --- lines:
  [scratch:///notes.md, lines 1-5]
  <content>
  ---
  [scratch:///other.md]
  <content>

reads.description: List of read requests. Each item specifies a file and an optional line range.

uri.description: The scratch URI to read from. Must be a valid scratch:/// URI or URI string.

lineFrom.description: Optional starting line number (1-based, inclusive). Matches line numbers from get_scratch_outline. If omitted, reads from the beginning.

lineTo.description: Optional ending line number (1-based, inclusive). Matches line numbers from get_scratch_outline. If omitted, reads to the end.

languageModelTools.name: get_scratch_outline
languageModelTools.userDescription: Returns a structured outline of symbols in a scratch file
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Returns a structured, indented outline of the symbols found in a scratch file: headings for Markdown, classes/methods/functions for code, etc.
Use to quickly understand the structure of a scratch without reading the full file. Ideal for identifying sections to target with read_scratch line ranges.

Parameters:
- uri (required): Scratch URI formed by prefixing a path from list_scratches with 'scratch:///' (e.g. if list_scratches returns 'notes.md', use 'scratch:///notes.md')
- depth (optional): Maximum nesting depth to include, minimum 1 (default: 2). depth=1 returns only top-level symbols (e.g. H1 headings, top-level classes); depth=2 also includes their direct children (e.g. H2 headings, class methods); etc.

Examples:
- { uri: "scratch:///notes.md" } → returns Markdown headings up to 2 levels deep
- { uri: "scratch:///code/app.ts", depth: 3 } → returns classes, their methods, and nested items
- { uri: "scratch:///data/config.json", depth: 1 } → returns only root-level JSON keys

Returns: symbols ordered top-to-bottom by file position, indented two spaces per level. Each entry reports name, kind, and line range ('line N' for single-line symbols, 'lines N-M' for multi-line):
  Introduction (String, line 1)
    Background (String, lines 3-12)
  Implementation (String, lines 15-30)
or 'No symbols found.' when the file type has no active symbol provider or the file is empty.

Notes:
- For flat scripts (JS/TS files without classes), every top-level variable declaration appears as a separate symbol at depth=1. This can produce a large number of entries; consider using depth=1 to see just the top-level list.
- On the first call for a file not yet opened in the current session, the language server may not have initialized yet; the tool retries once automatically.
- When a language server returns a flat SymbolInformation list instead of a hierarchical DocumentSymbol tree, depth filtering is applied via the containerName field. The result is accurate for most Markdown and simple code files, but may be approximate when symbol names are not unique within a file.

uri.description: The scratch URI to inspect. Must be a valid scratch:/// URI or URI string.

depth.description: Maximum nesting depth of symbols to include. depth=1 returns only top-level symbols; depth=2 also includes their direct children. Defaults to 2.

languageModelTools.name: write_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Writes content to one or more scratch files in a single call
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Writes content to one or more scratch files, creating them if they don't exist or overwriting if they do.
Use when user asks to save, create, or update scratch content. Prefer batching multiple writes into a single call.

Input: an object where each key is a scratch URI and its value is the content to write.

Examples:
- { "scratch:///notes.md": "My notes" } → creates/overwrites single file
- { "scratch:///a.md": "Content A", "scratch:///b.md": "Content B" } → writes two files at once
- { "scratch:///code/test.ts": "const x = 1;" } → creates nested file

Returns: "Scratches written successfully." on full success, or a list of failures. After writing always provide a short (1-2 lines) summary of changes made.

inputSchema.description: An object mapping scratch URIs to their content. Each key must be a valid scratch:/// URI or URI string; parent directories will be created if needed.

additionalProperties.description: The content to write to the scratch file identified by the key URI. Will overwrite existing content if the file exists.

languageModelTools.name: edit_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Applies granular edits to one or more scratch files
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Applies one or more granular line-level edits to existing scratch files. Supports insert, replace, and append operations, batched across multiple files in a single call.
Prefer over write_scratch when modifying part of a file — avoids rewriting content you haven't read.
Line numbers are 1-based physical lines (including blank lines), matching get_scratch_outline and read_scratch.

Parameters:
- edits (required): Array of per-file edit groups, each with:
  - uri (required): Scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///notes.md")
  - edits (required): Array of operations to apply to this file. Multiple ops on the same file are applied bottom-to-top automatically, so you can pass them in natural top-to-bottom order.
    - insert: { op: "insert", line: N, content: "..." } — inserts content before physical line N (1-based). Use line = (last line + 1) to insert after the last line, equivalent to append.
    - replace: { op: "replace", lineFrom: N, lineTo: M, content: "..." } — replaces physical lines N through M inclusive with content. Pass content: "" to delete the range.
    - append: { op: "append", content: "..." } — appends content after the last line of the file.

Examples:
- Insert a new section before line 10:
  { edits: [{ uri: "scratch:///notes.md", edits: [{ op: "insert", line: 10, content: "## New Section" }] }] }
- Replace lines 5-8 with updated content:
  { edits: [{ uri: "scratch:///notes.md", edits: [{ op: "replace", lineFrom: 5, lineTo: 8, content: "updated line" }] }] }
- Delete lines 3-5:
  { edits: [{ uri: "scratch:///code.ts", edits: [{ op: "replace", lineFrom: 3, lineTo: 5, content: "" }] }] }
- Append a footer and fix a heading in the same file:
  { edits: [{ uri: "scratch:///notes.md", edits: [{ op: "replace", lineFrom: 1, lineTo: 1, content: "# Fixed Title" }, { op: "append", content: "---\nEnd of file" }] }] }
- Edit two files at once:
  { edits: [{ uri: "scratch:///a.md", edits: [{ op: "append", content: "note" }] }, { uri: "scratch:///b.md", edits: [{ op: "insert", line: 1, content: "# Header" }] }] }

Returns: "Edited: path1, path2" on success, or an error if a file does not exist.

uri.description: The scratch URI to edit. Must be a valid scratch:/// URI or URI string. The file must already exist.

edits.description: Operations to apply to this file. Multiple ops are applied bottom-to-top so you can write them in natural reading order.

op.description: Inserts content before a given line.

line.description: Physical line number (1-based) to insert before. Use N+1 (where N is the file length) to insert after the last line. Values larger than N+1 are rejected.

content.description: Text to insert. May contain newlines for multi-line content.

op.description: Replaces a range of lines with new content. Pass content: "" to delete the range.

lineFrom.description: First physical line of the range to replace (1-based, inclusive).

lineTo.description: Last physical line of the range to replace (1-based, inclusive).

content.description: Replacement text. May contain newlines. Pass "" to delete the range.

op.description: Appends content after the last line of the file. Empty string is a no-op.

content.description: Text to append. May contain newlines. Empty string is treated as a no-op.

languageModelTools.name: rename_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Renames or moves a scratch file
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Renames or moves a scratch file from one URI to another.
Use when user asks to rename or move a scratch file to a new location or name.

NOTE: This tool modifies the file system and cannot be undone. Always confirm the old and new URIs with the user before proceeding.

Parameters:
- oldUri (required): Current scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///notes.md") or URI string
- newUri (required): New scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///renamed-notes.md") or URI string

Examples:
- { oldUri: "scratch:///notes.md", newUri: "scratch:///notes-backup.md" } → renames file
- { oldUri: "scratch:///misc/todo.txt", newUri: "scratch:///lists/todo.txt" } → moves file

Returns: "Scratch renamed successfully."

oldUri.description: The current scratch URI to rename from. Must be a valid scratch:/// URI or URI string.

newUri.description: The new scratch URI to rename to. Must be a valid scratch:/// URI or URI string. Parent directories will be created if needed.

languageModelTools.name: search_scratches
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Searches across scratch file contents using text or regex patterns, returning matches with context lines.
Use when user asks to find specific content, patterns, or references across scratches.

Parameters:
- query (required): Search term or regex pattern
- isRegex (optional): Treat query as regex (default: false)
- caseSensitive (optional): Case-sensitive search (default: false)
- filter (optional): Glob pattern, bare basename, path prefix, or scratch:/// URI to limit scope.
  - Glob (e.g. "**/*.md", "*.md", "projects/**"): patterns without a leading "**/" are prepended with it and match recursively.
  - Bare basename (no "/", e.g. "README.md"): expanded to "**/name" and matches files anywhere in the tree.
  - Path prefix (contains "/", e.g. "projects/foo"): restricts search to that directory subtree.
  - scratch:/// URI: scheme is stripped and the appropriate rule above is applied.
- contextLines (optional): Lines before/after to include (default: 2)
- maxResults (optional): Maximum matches to return (default: 100)

Examples:
- { query: "authentication" } → finds all occurrences with 2 lines context
- { query: "TODO|FIXME", isRegex: true } → finds TODOs using regex
- { query: "function", filter: "projects/my-app" } → finds in all files under projects/my-app/
- { query: "function", filter: "scratch:///projects/my-app" } → same, using scratch:/// URI
- { query: "function", filter: "**/*.ts", contextLines: 0 } → finds in TypeScript files, no context

Returns: formatted text listing each match as "path:line → content" with surrounding context lines indented by two spaces.

query.description: The search term or regex pattern. Required.

isRegex.description: Whether to treat the query as a regex pattern. Default: false.

caseSensitive.description: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Default: false.

filter.description: Glob pattern (e.g. "**/*.md", "*.md"), bare basename (e.g. "README.md" — matched anywhere in the tree), path prefix (e.g. "projects/foo" — restricts to that directory), or scratch:/// URI to limit search scope. Glob patterns and bare basenames without a leading **/ are automatically matched recursively.

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to include before and after each match. Default: 2.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Default: 100.

scratches.scratchDirectory.description: Directory where scratch files are stored, defaults to extension's global storage directory

viewsWelcome.contents:
No scratches found
[Create a new one](command:scratches.newScratch)

scripts.test:ui: npm run compile && npx extest run-tests 'out/ui-test/**/*.test.js' --storage .uitest-temp/ --code_version max --type stable --extensions_dir .uitest-temp/extensions
scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:ui
